mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate directly reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. When FEDFUNDS falls below its 3-month moving average, equity risk premiums compress and forward S&P 500 returns increase over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.38% as of June 2016 near historic lows
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in < 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS above 150k jobs predicts positive 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained job growth
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly capture consumer demand strength. When both series rise simultaneously above their 6-month trend, equity valuations expand due to improved earnings visibility. This interaction effect is stronger than either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both > 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 456978M and HOUST at 1164k as of May 2016
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either component alone.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. Sustained declines in INDPRO signal demand weakness and margin compression, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking equity prices adjust.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This triggers a negative equity risk premium adjustment, producing negative 20-day forward returns when UNRATE crosses above its 3-month average.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary pressure
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real interest rates. When inflation rises while FEDFUNDS remains flat or declines, real rates fall and equity valuations expand. This interaction drives positive 60-day forward returns in low-rate regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 239.93 and FEDFUNDS at 0.38% as of June 2016
regime_hint: low-rate monetary accommodation with modest inflation
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10), or positive 60-day returns occur in < 55% of joint-trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly reflects aggregate demand strength. When GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 2.5%, equity earnings expectations rise, producing positive 60-day forward returns. This is an always-on trend signal with no activation threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16514.6B (2017 dollars) as of Q1 2016
regime_hint: any regime with positive trend growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns occur in < 53% of all periods regardless of GDPC1 level.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal productive capacity and labor utilization. When both rise together, supply-side strength supports margin expansion and equity valuations. This interaction is regime-dependent: strongest in mid-cycle expansions, weaker in late-cycle.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 1-month prior AND PAYEMS monthly increase > 100000
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.13 and PAYEMS at 144175k as of June 2016
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized supply and labor growth
failure_condition: Interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or positive 20-day returns occur in < 54% of joint-trigger instances over rolling 2-year windows.
